2605.03301.title: SHIELD: A Diverse Clinical Note Dataset and Distilled Small Language Models for Enterprise-Scale De-identification
2605.03301.paper_summary: De-identification of clinical text remains essential for secondary use of electronic health records (EHRs), yet public benchmarks such as i2b2 2006/2014 are over a decade old and lack the semantic and demographic diversity of modern narratives. While Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve state-of-the-art zero-shot extraction, enterprise deployment is hindered by compute costs and governance restricting Protected Health Information (PHI) from cloud APIs. We introduce SHIELD (Synthetic Human-annotated Identifier-replaced Entries for Learning and De-identification), a diverse dataset of 1,394 notes with 10,505 gold-standard PHI spans across 9 categories, built via set-cover diversity sampling with human-in-the-loop adjudication. We evaluate four LLMs (two proprietary, two open-weight) to establish a performance ceiling, then distill these capabilities into locally deployable Small Language Models (SLMs). Distributional analysis using Frechet Text Distance and Jensen-Shannon Divergence confirms SHIELD occupies a distinct region of biomedical embedding and vocabulary space versus legacy benchmarks. Our best distilled model matches its teacher on structured PHI categories (DATE, DOCTOR, ID, PATIENT, PHONE) and achieves micro-averaged span-level precision of 0.88 and recall of 0.86 on standard workstation hardware. Cross-dataset evaluation shows diversity-trained models generalize well on universal structured PHI, while institution-specific entities remain hard to transfer, suggesting optimal deployment combines broad-coverage models with specialized models for high-volume notes. We publicly release the SHIELD dataset and the distilled DeBERTa v3 model.
2605.03301.paper_summary_zh: 臨床文本的去識別化對於電子健康記錄（EHRs）的二次使用仍然至關重要，但公共基準如i2b2 2006/2014已經超過十年，且缺乏現代敘事的語義和人口統計多樣性。雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在零樣本提取方面達到了最先進的水平，但企業部署受到計算成本和治理的限制，無法將受保護的健康信息（PHI）從雲端API中提取。我們推出了SHIELD（合成人工標註的標識符替換條目，用於學習和去識別化），這是一個包含1,394條筆記的多樣化數據集，擁有10,505個金標準PHI範圍，涵蓋9個類別，通過集合覆蓋多樣性抽樣和人類參與的裁定構建而成。我們評估了四個LLM（兩個專有的，兩個開放權重的），以確立性能上限，然後將這些能力提煉成可本地部署的小型語言模型（SLMs）。使用Frechet文本距離和Jensen-Shannon散度的分佈分析確認SHIELD在生物醫學嵌入和詞彙空間中佔據了與傳統基準不同的區域。我們最佳的提煉模型在結構化PHI類別（日期、醫生、ID、病人、電話）上與其教師相匹配，並在標準工作站硬件上達到了0.88的微平均範圍精確度和0.86的召回率。跨數據集評估顯示，多樣性訓練的模型在通用結構化PHI上具有良好的泛化能力，而特定機構的實體則難以轉移，這表明最佳部署應將廣泛覆蓋的模型與針對高容量筆記的專門模型相結合。我們公開發布了SHIELD數據集和提煉的DeBERTa v3模型。
2605.03301.author: Jose D. Posada et.al.
2605.03301.authors: Jose D. Posada, David Love, Somalee Datta, Priya Desai
